title: Follow Rails conventions over configuration
description: Name and place code where Rails expects so autoloading, routing, and associations work without manual wiring.
body: Use when adding models, controllers, or routes. Do not fight Rails' naming and directory conventions or hand-wire what convention provides. Actions: name files and classes so Zeitwerk autoloads them (snake_case file, CamelCase class), place them in the expected app/ subdirectory, pluralize controllers and use RESTful resources routes, and rely on convention-based association/table naming. Validation signals include the class autoloading with no require and the route resolving in a request spec. Risk boundary: deviating from convention forces manual requires and brittle config that break on upgrade; a class that fails to autoload because it violates naming is a defect to rename, not to patch with eager requires.
